pr.title: Split up more of the ft files into their smaller counterparts
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Mechanical fighter assembly reorganization: the large asm/melee/ft/code_800804EC.s block was split/renamed into smaller ft assembly files, with semantic filenames for known common areas such as ftdrawcommon, ftcliffcommon, ftwalljump, and ftcommon, plus address-based files for remaining unknown ranges. obj_files.mk was updated to replace the single old object with the new object list so link order and build coverage stayed explicit.
what_changed: Renamed asm/melee/ft/code_800804EC.s to asm/melee/ft/code_8008521C.s and removed most of its earlier function bodies from that remainder file.
Added new asm files for split-out ranges: code_80081938.s, code_80081B38.s, ftdrawcommon.s, ftcliffcommon.s, and ftwalljump.s.
Moved func_800804EC and func_800804FC into the existing asm/melee/ft/ftcommon.s file.

Moved functions beginning at func_8008051C into asm/melee/ft/ftdrawcommon.s, functions beginning at func_80081298 into asm/melee/ft/ftcliffcommon.s, and the 0x80081938/0x80081B38 ranges into separate address-named files.
Updated obj_files.mk by replacing $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/ft/code_800804EC.o with ftdrawcommon.o, ftcliffcommon.o, ftwalljump.o, code_80081938.o, code_80081B38.o, and code_8008521C.o.
smart_moves: Kept semantically understood fighter code in named files like ftcommon.s, ftdrawcommon.s, ftcliffcommon.s, and ftwalljump.s while retaining address-based names for less-understood leftovers.
Preserved the original contiguous object coverage by listing all new split objects in obj_files.mk at the old code_800804EC.o position, reducing risk of accidental link-order drift.
Carried over .section address comments, global labels, and sdata2 constants with the moved assembly chunks, which is important for exact assembly rebuilds.
Used a minimal mechanical split rather than mixing reorganization with C decompilation, making review and regression risk smaller.
decomp_lessons: Large monolithic asm files can be safely prepared for future decompilation by splitting them along function/range boundaries before attempting C matches.
When a range has a known subsystem purpose, prefer a semantic filename; when confidence is weak, keep address-based names instead of over-naming.
Every asm split must be mirrored in obj_files.mk, and the new object order should match the original address/link order as closely as possible.

Small helper functions near a semantic boundary can be moved into the adjacent existing common file when the address placement and context support it, as with func_800804EC and func_800804FC moving into ftcommon.s.

For assembly-only splits, evidence of correctness is mostly structural: identical function bodies, preserved labels/constants, and build-list replacement; there was no PR body or review discussion to infer deeper intent.
naming_conventions: Use ft-prefixed semantic filenames for fighter common subsystems when the role is known: ftcommon.s, ftdrawcommon.s, ftcliffcommon.s, ftwalljump.s.
Use code_XXXXXXXX.s address filenames for still-unknown or broad leftover ranges, as shown by code_80081938.s, code_80081B38.s, and code_8008521C.s.
Preserve generated symbol names such as func_80081938, func_80081B38, lbl_80081B20, and lbl_804D83A0 when no better decompiled names are established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split assembly at function boundaries while retaining .include "macros.inc" and explicit .section declarations in each new file.
Keep global function labels and local branch labels unchanged so cross-file branch/call references remain resolvable.
Move associated .sdata2 constants with the assembly chunk that references them, such as lbl_804D83A0/lbl_804D83A4 in code_80081938.s and lbl_804D8378/lbl_804D8380 in ftcliffcommon.s.
Maintain object ordering in obj_files.mk to preserve the original text layout after replacing one large object with several smaller objects.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR slice.
The PR merged immediately after creation, so there is no recorded reviewer rationale beyond the title and diff.
searchable_terms: Split up more of the ft files into their smaller counterparts
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile functions moved into ftdrawcommon.s, especially func_8008051C and neighboring 0x800805xx functions.
Find later PRs that rename or decompile ftcliffcommon.s functions beginning at func_80081298.
Find later PRs touching code_80081B38.s or code_8008521C.s to see how the remaining address-named ranges were eventually classified.
Check whether object order in obj_files.mk remained stable after additional ft assembly splits.
